design_philosophy.core_loop: Shoot waves of enemies entering from arena edges while collecting money/prizes/power-ups, then choose an exit leading to next arena. Clear all enemies to progress.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Survival against escalating enemy waves in confined single-screen arenas; resource scarcity (health/ammunition) vs. reward collection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-stakes competition on a dystopian game show; accumulate wealth and prizes while surviving combat; competitive ego when playing cooperatively (end-of-level ranking by earnings)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Arcade-era philosophy: designed to punish and demand continued payment. Twin-stick control scheme enforces simultaneous movement and aiming as core skill expression. Cooperative gameplay undermined by competitive scoring—rewards individual performance within shared space. Dystopian entertainment spectacle as narrative framing legitimizes violence-for-profit.

difficulty.lives_system: Arcade continue system; home console versions adapt this structure
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade designed around paid continues; home versions retain high difficulty scaling to encourage replays
difficulty.checkpointing: Single-screen arena progression; no mid-level saves. Level map reveals path to boss after first arena completion, but no backtracking allowed
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select (cheat code) on SNES allows difficulty customization and arena selection. Power-ups and weapon variety provide tactical resource management within fixed difficulty curve

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups and weapons collected mid-arena (money, prizes, weapon variants). Keys collected across arenas unlock bonus 'Pleasure Dome' level if threshold met
progression.unlock_mechanism: Key collection gates access to bonus content. Level select cheat code unlocks arbitrary arena/boss access and credits
replayability_hooks: Competitive scoring system encourages replays to outperform co-op partner
Multiple exits per arena create branching paths (though all lead to same pool of possible arenas)
Pleasure Dome bonus level gates behind key collection
Hidden sound test and developer credits reward exploration
Arcade-derived 'one more run' structure with increasing challenge

world_building_techniques: Dystopian game show setting (future 1999) frames all action as televised spectacle
Announcer voice-over provides commentary ('Total carnage! I love it!', 'Big money! Big prizes!') reinforcing entertainment framing
Prize items (VCRs, toasters, 'babes') are collectible and scored, treating survival commodities as game show rewards
Final boss is sadistic host character, making narrative climax confrontation with authority figure
RoboCop and The Running Man thematic borrowing positions this as dystopian satire of entertainment and violence

boss_design.philosophy: End-of-level boss encounters cap each arena progression. Boss appears as location marker on level map, signaling narrative climax. Final boss (sadistic host) represents thematic antagonist.

faction_indicators: Cooperative players vs. competitive scoring creates internal alliance tension
Game show framework positions players as commodified entertainment products
danger_signals: Objectification of female characters ('bikini-clad babes' as collectible prizes in Pleasure Dome)
Violence-as-entertainment celebration ('Total carnage! I love it!' announcer approval)
Dystopian framing that treats human survival as spectator sport commodity
environmental_storytelling: Arena passages suggest broadcast set design; enclosed spaces imply stadium containment
Money and prize pickups visualize materialist reward structure
Single-screen arena limitation creates 'arena combat' aesthetic borrowed from gladiatorial spectacle

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Super Smash T.V. SNES)
